# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

function generateReport(array $postData): array
{
    // --- Database connection ---
    // In a real application, you would typically have a separate configuration
    // file or a service locator to manage your database connection.
    // For this example, we'll hardcode it.
    $dbHost = 'localhost';
    $dbName = 'your_database_name';
    $dbUser = 'your_username';
    $dbPass = 'your_password';
    $charset = 'utf8mb4';

    try {
        $dsn = "mysql:host=$dbHost;dbname=$dbName;charset=$charset";
        $pdo = new PDO($dsn, $dbUser, $dbPass, [
            PDO::ATTR_ERRMODE            => PDO::ERRMODE_EXCEPTION,
            PDO::ATTR_DEFAULT_FETCH_MODE => PDO::FETCH_ASSOC,
            PDO::ATTR_EMULATE_PREPARES   => false,
        ]);
    } catch (PDOException $e) {
        // Log the error or handle it gracefully in a production environment
        error_log("Database connection error: " . $e->getMessage());
        return ['error' => 'Database connection failed.'];
    }

    // --- Dynamic SQL Query Construction ---
    $sql = "SELECT id, user_id, amount, status, category, transaction_date, description FROM transactions";
    $conditions = [];
    $params = [];

    // Filter by Status
    if (!empty($postData['status'])) {
        $conditions[] = "status = :status";
        $params[':status'] = $postData['status'];
    }

    // Filter by Category
    if (!empty($postData['category'])) {
        $conditions[] = "category = :category";
        $params[':category'] = $postData['category'];
    }

    // Filter by Date Range
    $startDate = $postData['start_date'] ?? null;
    $endDate = $postData['end_date'] ?? null;

    if (!empty($startDate) && !empty($endDate)) {
        // Ensure the full day is included for both start and end dates
        $conditions[] = "transaction_date BETWEEN :start_date AND :end_date";
        $params[':start_date'] = $startDate . ' 00:00:00';
        $params[':end_date'] = $endDate . ' 23:59:59';
    } elseif (!empty($startDate)) {
        $conditions[] = "transaction_date >= :start_date";
        $params[':start_date'] = $startDate . ' 00:00:00';
    } elseif (!empty($endDate)) {
        $conditions[] = "transaction_date <= :end_date";
        $params[':end_date'] = $endDate . ' 23:59:59';
    }

    // Assemble the WHERE clause
    if (!empty($conditions)) {
        $sql .= " WHERE " . implode(" AND ", $conditions);
    }

    // Optional: Add ORDER BY clause for better readability of reports
    $sql .= " ORDER BY transaction_date DESC";

    // --- Execute the Query ---
    try {
        $stmt = $pdo->prepare($sql);
        $stmt->execute($params);
        $reportData = $stmt->fetchAll(PDO::FETCH_ASSOC);

        return $reportData;

    } catch (PDOException $e) {
        // Log the error and return an error message
        error_log("Report generation error: " . $e->getMessage() . " SQL: " . $sql . " Params: " . json_encode($params));
        return ['error' => 'Failed to generate report: ' . $e->getMessage()];
    }
}

// Example Usage (simulate $_POST data)
/*
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    $reportResults = generateReport($_POST);

    if (isset($reportResults['error'])) {
        echo "Error: " . $reportResults['error'];
    } else {
        echo "<pre>";
        print_r($reportResults);
        echo "</pre>";
        // You would typically format this into an HTML table or CSV for download
    }
} else {
    // This is where you'd display your HTML form for filters
    echo "<form method='POST'>";
    echo "Status: <input type='text' name='status'><br>";
    echo "Category: <input type='text' name='category'><br>";
    echo "Start Date: <input type='date' name='start_date'><br>";
    echo "End Date: <input type='date' name='end_date'><br>";
    echo "<button type='submit'>Generate Report</button>";
    echo "</form>";
}
*/
?>
